mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate levels combined with elevated inflation compress real policy rates, and markets anticipate a future tightening cycle. This anticipatory repricing of discount rates historically depresses S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon even before hikes materialize.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals labor market overheating, raising the probability of accelerated Fed tightening and wage-driven inflation, which compresses equity valuations over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% having declined more than 0.5pp over the trailing 6 months
failure_condition: Post-trigger 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation exceeds 6% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains near zero, the resulting deeply negative real policy rate signals an imminent aggressive tightening cycle. Markets pre-price higher future discount rates, producing negative forward equity returns that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 6% while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Joint-condition periods show non-negative mean 20-day forward returns, or the interaction term contributes less than 0.5% incremental R-squared versus an additive single-factor model.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining sharply at the outset of a Fed tightening cycle act as a leading indicator of broader demand deceleration, historically foreshadowing weaker corporate earnings growth and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over a trailing 3-month window
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening periods are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns, or the relationship reverses sign in more than two of the last five tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding real output and corporate revenue capacity, leading investors to revise earnings expectations upward and driving positive forward equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Periods of positive INDPRO momentum show forward returns not significantly above the zero-mean baseline, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in retail sales signals robust near-term consumer demand, which short-horizon equity markets reward within days as analysts revise near-term revenue forecasts higher.
failure_condition: Triggered instances fail to produce a positive mean 5-day return that is statistically greater than the unconditional 5-day mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a very low unemployment rate jointly signals a labor market operating beyond sustainable capacity, raising unit labor cost inflation risk and prompting tighter monetary policy expectations that weigh on equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 2% while UNRATE is below 4%
failure_condition: Joint high-payroll-growth/low-unemployment periods do not show below-baseline 60-day returns, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where policy rates have been held near zero for an extended period, a sustained breach of 5% CPI inflation signals loss of price stability control, triggering repricing of the rate-hike path and negative equity returns over the following month; this relationship is expected to be weaker outside near-zero-rate regimes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 5% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Within the near-zero-rate regime, triggered periods do not show statistically significant negative 20-day forward returns, or the effect is equally strong outside this regime.
